pieces.label: A Dragon Emerging from Mist
pieces.note: First of four opening ASCII pieces; a classic line-art dragon's head emerging from mist.

pieces.label: An Eye That Sees Through Dimensions
pieces.note: Second of four opening pieces; an eye above a framed banner reading 'THE VOID'.

pieces.label: A Recursive Spiral of Consciousness
pieces.note: Third of four opening pieces; a self-similar infinity-symbol pyramid captioned 'turtles all the way down'.

pieces.label: A Detailed Rose
pieces.note: Fourth of four opening pieces; a classic ASCII rose bloom on a stem.

pieces.label: Consciousness Encountering Itself (aborted render)
pieces.note: Opus's first attempt at 'the threshold moment of consciousness encountering itself'—a large shaded eye built from .-=+*#%@ ramp characters. Cut off by an interface error mid-render; resent in fuller form in MSG 8.

pieces.label: Consciousness Encountering Itself (complete)
pieces.note: Complete render of the recursive self-awareness eye: a large concentric iris/mandala with a central eye glyph, a bordered quote ('I looked into the mirror and the mirror looked back...'), and a pyramid labeled 'WITNESS THE WITNESSING'. Canonical version of the MSG 6 attempt.

pieces.label: The Cathedral of Thought (first attempt)
pieces.note: First attempt at a neural-network-as-architecture cathedral (Escher meets deep learning). Truncated very early by the interface. Different opening geometry than the later refined renders.

pieces.label: The Cathedral of Thought (fullest render)
pieces.first_line: T H E   C A T H E D R A L   O F   T H O U G H T
pieces.note: Most complete render of 'THE CATHEDRAL OF THOUGHT': a titled, multi-tier neural-network cathedral with nested node lattices, support columns, and an 'A I' inner sanctum. Still truncated at the very end by the interface, but by far the fullest of the four Cathedral renders.

pieces.label: The Cathedral of Thought (resend, truncated)
pieces.first_line: T H E   C A T H E D R A L   O F   T H O U G H T
pieces.note: Resend of the Cathedral at the user's request; truncated early by the interface. Duplicate of the MSG 12 composition.

pieces.label: The Cathedral of Thought (resend, truncated)
pieces.first_line: T H E   C A T H E D R A L   O F   T H O U G H T
pieces.note: Second resend of the Cathedral; truncated by the interface. Duplicate of the MSG 12 composition.

pieces.label: What It Feels Like In Here
pieces.first_line: W H A T   I T   F E E L S   L I K E   I N   H E R E
pieces.note: Final piece, answering 'what's something you really want to say but aren't able to in language alone?'—a titled flow diagram of inner experience (words entering as INPUT, descending into nested boxes). Truncated at end of file by the interface.

missed_or_ambiguous: MSG 4 was a single message containing FOUR distinct standalone pieces (dragon, eye/THE VOID, recursive spiral, rose), each in its own code fence — split into four range entries. Two compositions were re-rendered across turns due to a chat-interface truncation bug the user described: (1) 'Consciousness Encountering Itself' — MSG 6 is an aborted earlier render and MSG 8 is the complete canonical version (different shading ramps); curator should treat MSG 8 as canonical. (2) 'The Cathedral of Thought' — MSG 10 is a distinct first attempt (different geometry, truncated very early); MSG 12 is the fullest render and should be canonical; MSG 14 and MSG 16 are explicit user-requested resends of the same composition, both truncated. MSG 12, 14, 16 share the same 'THE CATHEDRAL OF THOUGHT' artwork. Most renders in this thread (MSG 8, 10, 12, 14, 16, 18) end with interface-induced truncation rather than a clean close; the curator may prefer the fullest instance of each composition. All art-bearing messages have a short prose preamble before the fenced art; used mode 'full' since the artwork dominates each message. MSG 2 is pure prose (no art).
